alignments.preprint_claim_text: Tensor networks have been established as a successful machine learning paradigm.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Tensor networks have been established as a successful machine learning paradigm.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same concluding statement with identical meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Tensor networks can be deployed on quantum computers due to their nature at the interface between physics and machine learning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Tensor networks can be deployed on quantum computers due to their nature at the interface between physics and machine learning.
alignments.rationale: The claim is carried over verbatim, preserving meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Layouts like MPS, PEPS, TTNs, and MERA can be mapped to a quantum computer for machine learning and data encoding.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Layouts like matrix product state, projected entangled pair states, tree tensor networks, and multi-scale entanglement renormalization ansatz can be mapped to a quantum computer.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same set of tensor-network layouts (with expanded full names) and the same mapping claim; meaning is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Implementation techniques can improve the performance of tensor networks in quantum machine learning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The implementation techniques discussed can improve the performance of tensor networks in quantum machine learning.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same result about implementation techniques improving performance with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed online learning approach for wheel odometry correction paves the way for a robust multi-source localization system.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed online learning approach for wheel odometry correction paves the way for a robust multi-source localization system.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this contribution verbatim, preserving scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The efficient attention-based neural network architecture combines precise performances with real-time inference.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The efficient attention-based neural network architecture combines precise performances with realtime inference.
alignments.rationale: The result is carried over unchanged in wording and meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed solution shows remarkable results compared to a standard neural network and filter-based odometry correction algorithms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed solution shows remarkable results compared to a standard neural network and filter-based odometry correction algorithms.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the comparative performance claim without modification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The online learning paradigm avoids the time-consuming data collection procedure and can be adopted on a generic robotic platform on-the-fly.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The online learning paradigm avoids the time-consuming data collection procedure and can be adopted on a generic robotic platform on-the-fly.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about on-the-fly adoption is retained exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method for stepwise explaining solutions of Constraint Satisfaction Problems generates explanations that are provably optimal with respect to a given cost metric.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method generates explanations that are provably optimal with respect to a given cost metric.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the core claim of provable optimality but lists it with lower stated certainty (medium vs high), so the claim content is unchanged but confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed hitting set-based algorithm finds optimal constrained unsatisfiable subsets efficiently.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed algorithms outperform the MUS approach in terms of explanation quality and computational time.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the preprint's specific hitting-set/efficiency claim as a more general statement that the developed algorithms outperform MUS in quality and time; this clarifies and generalizes the original implementation-focused claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithms outperform the Minimal Unsatisfiable Subsets approach in terms of explanation quality and computational time, being on average up to 56% faster.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithms are on average up to 56% faster than a standard MUS approach.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the quantitative speedup claim directly (up to 56% faster), matching the preprint's result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SSL-GANs achieve near identical results to OSR-GANs, proving the SSL-OSR link.
alignments.pub_claim_text: SSL-GANs achieve near identical results to OSR-GANs, proving the SSL-OSR link.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this central empirical result verbatim, preserving meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The definitions of bad-looking samples in SSL and OSR represent the same concept and realize the same goal.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate or discuss this specific claim equating the definitions; it appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: By regularising a classifier with samples in the complementary space, classifiers achieve improved generalisation for SSL and also generalise the open space for OSR.
alignments.pub_claim_text: By regularising a classifier with samples in the complementary space, classifiers achieve improved generalisation for SSL and also generalise the open space for OSR.
alignments.rationale: The publication contains the same conceptual claim but lists it with slightly lower stated certainty (medium vs high), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A combined framework of SSL-OSR certainly improves the practicality and cost-efficiency of classifier training.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A combined framework of SSL-OSR certainly improves the practicality and cost-efficiency of classifier training.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents this contribution with higher stated certainty (high) than the preprint (medium), so the confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We demonstrate online training of deep ResNet-like models on up to a few million unique on-the-fly generated synthetic diffractograms per hour.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include this specific throughput/training demonstration detail; that operational claim was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: For our chosen task of space group classification, we achieved a test accuracy of 79.9% on unseen ICSD structure types from most space groups.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The test accuracy achieved for space group classification is 79.9% on unseen ICSD structure types, surpassing the 56.1% accuracy of the current state-of-the-art approach.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the identical numeric accuracy for the stated evaluation, combining it with the SOTA comparison in the same claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This surpasses the 56.1% accuracy of the current state-of-the-art approach of training on ICSD crystals directly.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The test accuracy achieved for space group classification is 79.9% on unseen ICSD structure types, surpassing the 56.1% accuracy of the current state-of-the-art approach.
alignments.rationale: The publication contains the same comparative performance result, matched verbatim in the combined claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our results demonstrate that synthetically generated crystals can be used to extract structural information from ICSD powder diffractograms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Synthetically generated crystals can be used to extract structural information from ICSD powder diffractograms, enabling the application of large machine learning models in powder X-ray diffraction.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the core claim but expands it by explicitly linking the ability to extract structural information to enabling large ML models; this broadens and clarifies the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach has the potential to be extended to related tasks in the future.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The methodology has potential for extension to related tasks beyond space group prediction.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the future-extension potential with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We propose three novel evaluation schemes to measure the faithfulness of post-hoc attribution methods. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose three novel evaluation schemes to measure the faithfulness of post-hoc attribution methods. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution verbatim; there is no substantive change in meaning, scope, or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel evaluation setting (DiFull) allows us to distinguish possible from impossible attributions. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The DiFull evaluation setting allows us to distinguish possible from impossible attributions by controlling input influence. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same claim but adds a brief mechanism ('by controlling input influence'), which clarifies how DiFull achieves the distinction.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Evaluating all methods on the same layers (ML-Att) impacts their performance on quantitative metrics. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Evaluating all methods on the same layers (ML-Att) impacts their performance on quantitative metrics. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is carried over unchanged in the publication with the same scope and level of confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We propose a post-processing smoothing step that significantly improves the performance of some attribution methods. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose a post-processing smoothing step that significantly improves the performance of some attribution methods. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this contribution verbatim and with the same asserted impact.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach, named Dens-PU, effectively solves the PU learning problem using an anomaly-detection strategy. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: These results demonstrate the efficacy of Dense-PU in enhancing classification performance in identifying negative samples in unlabeled data. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The preprint asserts the method 'effectively solves' PU learning; the publication instead frames the outcome more conservatively as demonstrating efficacy in improving classification, which is a weakening/softening of the original absolute claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Latent encodings extracted from positive-labeled data are linearly combined to create new samples that enhance the density of positive-labeled data. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method uses a Convolutional Autoencoder to extract latent encodings from positive-labeled data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes CAE extraction of encodings but does not describe the linear-combination/sample-generation detail from the preprint, narrowing the methodological description.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Dens-PU approach defines a boundary that approximates the positive class based on the density of positive-labeled data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel approach for solving the PU learning problem is proposed based on an anomaly detection strategy. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the technique at a higher level (anomaly detection-based approach) which is compatible with the preprint's description of a density/boundary-based rule but omits the boundary-detail, so the claim is generalized/clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method attained state-of-the-art results when evaluated using benchmark image datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach was evaluated using benchmark image datasets, yielding F1-scores of 91.96% and 94.80% on CIFAR-10 and Fashion-MNIST respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides concrete benchmark results and explicit F1 scores that substantiate and strengthen the preprint's claim of strong performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Beam Management (BM) algorithm can significantly reduce the number of beam reselections or radio link failures compared to the baseline algorithm. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Beam Management (BM) algorithm can significantly reduce the number of beam reselections or radio link failures compared to the baseline algorithm. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same quantitative result claim with no substantive change.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The BM algorithm utilizes location-dependent data stored in a Radio Environment Map (REM) to optimize the BM process. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution is repeated with equivalent wording and scope in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed solution is compliant with the Open Radio Access Network (O-RAN) architecture, enabling its practical implementation. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning pipeline achieved a median Dice score of 0.91 for end-diastolic volume, 0.86 for end-systolic volume, and 0.74 for myocardial mass in the testing dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning pipeline demonstrated acceptable limits of agreement and minimal biases between the ground truth and deep learning end-diastolic volume and end-systolic volume. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There was no statistical difference between the deep learning-derived cardiac function values and those derived from manual segmentation, with p values all greater than 0.05. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning pipeline demonstrated acceptable limits of agreement and minimal biases between the ground truth and deep learning end-diastolic volume and end-systolic volume. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the specific claim about application to the 4500-exam FORCE registry; this specific deployment statement was omitted.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Some synthetic gesture conditions are rated as significantly more human-like than 3D human motion capture. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: All synthetic motion is found to be vastly less appropriate for the speech than the original motion-capture recordings. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Conventional objective metrics do not correlate well with subjective human-likeness ratings in this large evaluation. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Mirror is an open-source platform for data exploration and analysis powered by large language models. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The number of relaxation eigenmodes for a Monte Carlo algorithm can be surprisingly small, one or two, when the jump length is comparable to the size of the box. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The relaxation towards equilibrium in a Monte Carlo algorithm for a particle in a box is unusual, with the number of relaxation eigenmodes being surprisingly small, one or two, for jump lengths comparable to the size of the box. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: When only a single relaxation eigenmode is present, a suitable choice of the symmetry of the initial conditions leads to a localizing decay to equilibrium. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: When only a single relaxation eigenmode is present, a suitable choice of the symmetry of the initial conditions leads to a localizing decay to equilibrium. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The panoptic one-click segmentation approach is approximately 12 times faster to train compared to traditional one-click approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result verbatim; no substantive change in meaning, scope, or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The global model may deviate from the basin's center while still outperforming the client models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The global model may deviate from the basin's center while still outperforming the client models.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this conclusion without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The global model loss after early training mainly arises from the client model's loss on non-overlapping data between client datasets and the global dataset and the maximum distance between the global and client models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The global model loss after early training mainly arises from the client model's loss on non-overlapping data between client datasets and the global dataset and the maximum distance between the global and client models.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this analytical result identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Incorporating iterative moving averaging (IMA) into existing FL methods significantly improves their accuracy and training speed on various heterogeneous data setups of benchmark datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Incorporating iterative moving averaging (IMA) into existing FL methods significantly improves their accuracy and training speed on various heterogeneous data setups of benchmark datasets.
alignments.rationale: The empirical claim about IMA's benefits is carried over unchanged into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MetaMorphosis is a novel deep learning-based privacy-cognizant feature generation process that limits inference capability to specific tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MetaMorphosis is a novel deep learning-based privacy-cognizant feature generation process that limits inference capability to specific tasks.
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is reproduced in the publication with the same framing and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Cross-SEC module achieves distinct attention for all tasks in the feature generation process.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Cross-SEC module achieves distinct attention for all tasks in the feature generation process.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this technical contribution claim directly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MetaMorphosis outperforms recent adversarial learning and universal feature generation methods by guaranteeing privacy requirements for image and video analytics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MetaMorphosis outperforms recent adversarial learning and universal feature generation methods by guaranteeing privacy requirements for image and video analytics.
alignments.rationale: The result claim about outperforming baselines with privacy guarantees is preserved in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep features generated by traditional multi-task learning may violate input and task privacy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The generated universal features can expose collective information intended for specific tasks, violating task privacy.
alignments.rationale: The preprint raised both input and task privacy concerns; the publication separates and clarifies the task-privacy aspect specifically in Q3.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep features generated may inadvertently contain input information exposed to downstream modules, violating input privacy.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly states the input-privacy concern as a distinct limitation; the preprint mentioned both concerns jointly, but the publication presents this as an additional, separately articulated claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ProKnow-guided method generated 89% safer questions in the depression and anxiety domain.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ProKnow-algo generated 89% safer questions in the depression and anxiety domain.
alignments.rationale: The reported safety improvement (89%) is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed ProKnow-guided methods achieved an average 82% improvement over simple pre-trained LMs on safety, explainability, and process-guided question generation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ProKnow-algo achieved an averaged 82% improvement over simple pre-trained LMs on safety, explainability, and process-guided question generation.
alignments.rationale: The averaged 82% improvement claim is carried into the publication without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The limitations of using state-of-the-art large-scale language models on the dataset were demonstrated.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly report a claim about demonstrating limitations of SOTA LMs on the dataset; that specific limitation statement is not present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Three new evaluation metrics for safety, explainability, and process knowledge adherence were introduced to evaluate the efficacy of the proposed methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports evaluation results but does not explicitly claim the introduction of three new metrics as stated in the preprint; thus the contribution claim appears removed or unstated.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Without ProKnow-algo, generated questions did not adhere to clinical process knowledge in ProKnow-data.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an explicit claim that generations without the algorithm fail to adhere to clinical process knowledge; this specific negative baseline statement was not present in the preprint's enumerated claims.

alignments.pub_claim_text: ProKnow-algo-based generations yield a 96% reduction in metrics measuring knowledge capture.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a specific 96% reduction metric not stated in the preprint's listed claims, so this is treated as an added publication-level quantitative result.
